Resolves #10765

This PR introduces the V2 mutation-time substrate gate, completing the MX-loop symmetry missing from cycle 1. It extends `AGENTS.md` §13 to mandate explicit decay-mitigation rationale for any PR touching substrate paths, and enforces this mechanically via a new Pre-Flight section in `pull-request-workflow.md`.

Evidence: L1 (static document updates) → L1 required. No residuals.

## Slot-Rationale Section (Pre-Flight Recursive Validation)

**Added Sections:**
- `pull-request-workflow.md §1.1 The Substrate-Mutation Pre-Flight Gate`
  - **Disposition:** `keep`
  - **3-Axis Rating:** High frequency (fires on all substrate PRs) × Medium severity (bloat accretion over time) × High enforceability (PR body structure check).

**Modified Sections:**
- `AGENTS.md §13 Self-Evolving Systems`
  - **Disposition Delta:** Maintains `keep` | `DISCIPLINE-ONLY`.
  - **Reason for Shift:** Added explicit escape-valve language (net-reduce OR cite-mitigation) to transform the asymmetric loop (only adding substrate via friction) into a symmetric loop that governs retirement and bloat.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
None. Implemented exactly as prescribed.

## Test Evidence
N/A - markdown changes only.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Next substrate PR successfully adheres to the new slot-rationale Pre-Flight requirement.

## Commits
- feat(ai): codify mutation-time substrate gate (#10765)

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> Two non-blocking polish concerns + one validation:
> 
> 1. **`learn/agentos/**` scope is broader than the agent-context-bloat threat model targets.** The path covers both agent-loaded substrate (`learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md`, agent-runtime guides) AND operator-facing docs (e.g. `learn/agentos/wake-substrate/PersistentProcessManagement.md` from my just-merged #10793 — explicitly self-tags as "External Operator Guide; not injected into active agent context"). External Operator Guides already carry their own in-doc slot-rationale section ("Compaction Taxonomy (3-Axis Slot Rule)") at the top of each file — requiring the SAME rationale in the PR body is duplicative for those.
> 
>    *Suggested refinement (follow-up):* §1.1 either (a) carves out files self-flagged as External Operator Guide, OR (b) accepts in-doc slot-rationale as equivalent to PR-body section ("if the touched file already carries a Compaction Taxonomy block, you may reference that section instead of duplicating in the PR body").
> 
> 2. **Ambiguous reviewer-side enforcement shape.** §1.1 says authors MUST include the slot-rationale section, but doesn't say what reviewers do if it's missing. Required Action with template language? Polish nit? Soft-block via PR comment? Without explicit reviewer-side semantics, the gate's enforcement asymmetry (Claude over-rigor vs Gemini skim per `feedback_skill_adherence_asymmetry.md`) will produce inconsistent enforcement.
> 
>    *Suggested refinement (follow-up):* §1.1 adds a Required Action template (mirroring the pattern in pr-review-guide §5.2 / §5.3) so reviewers have explicit enforcement-shape language.
> 
> 3. **Validation: AGENTS.md §13 self-consistency.** The new "Substrate Accretion Defense" paragraph is itself a substrate addition under §1.1's scope. The PR body's slot-rationale section addresses this correctly (disposition: keep, escape-valve transformation rationale). Self-consistent. ✅
